'''Jason Critchley''' (born 7 December 1970) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] footballer who played the 1980s, 1990s and 2000s. At club level for the [[Castleford Tigers]], [[Keighley Cougars]], [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]], [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]] and the [[Widnes Vikings]] as a {{rlp|wg}} or {{rlp|ce}},<ref name= "Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> and top level club level rugby union for [[Newport RFC]], [[Leicester Tigers]], [[Manchester Rugby Club|Manchester]] ([[Loan (sports)|loan]]), [[US Dax]] and [[De La Salle Palmerston]].

He played representative rugby league for Great Britain at every age level from under 16's, 19's and 21's. He was also selected for [[England national rugby league team|England]], [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] on the tour to Papua New Guinea, Fiji and New Zealand in 1996. 

==Personal Information==
Critchley was born in [[St Helens, Merseyside|St Helens]], [[Lancashire]], England.<ref>{{cite web |last1=de la Rivière |first1=Richard |title=Rugby League Heroes: Jason Critchley |url=https://www.totalrl.com/rugby-league-heroes-jason-critchley/ |website=Total Rugby League |access-date=11 May 2024 |date=15 November 2023}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Critchley signed for [[Widnes Vikings]] from junior club [[Blackbrook A.R.L.F.C.|Blackbrook]] in August 1989.<ref>{{cite book |editor-last1=Fletcher |editor-first1=Raymond |editor-last2=Howes |editor-first2=David |title=Rothmans Rugby League Yearbook 1990-91 |date=1990 |publisher=Queen Anne Press |location=London |isbn=978-0-356-17851-6 |page=386}}</ref> In 1992, he was sold to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] as part of an exchange deal for [[Adrian Hadley]].<ref>{{cite news |title=Sport in Brief |work=The Guardian |date=4 August 1992 |location=London |page=15 |id={{ProQuest|187241495}}}}</ref> Critchley was Salford's top try scorer in the [[1993–94 Rugby Football League season|1993–94 season]] with 25 tries.

Critchley joined [[Keighley Cougars]] in 1995. In August 1996, he scored six tries for Keighley Cougars in a match against Widnes Vikings, breaking a club record which had stood for 90 years for most tries in a single match.<ref>{{cite news |title=Leeds find scoring touch at last |work=The Guardian |date=19 August 1996 |location=London |page=12 |id={{ProQuest|187920731}}}}</ref> 

In May 1997, Critchley was signed by [[Castleford Tigers]] in exchange for [[Adrian Flynn]] and an undisclosed transfer fee.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Fitzpatrick |first1=Paul |title=Castleford swoop for Critchley the ambitious Cougar |work=The Guardian |date=17 May 1997 |location=London |page=A9 |id={{ProQuest|187976362}}}}</ref> Castleford were bottom of the [[Super League]], and hadn't won a single game when Critchley first arrived at the club, but he helped the club improve results during the rest of the season, and finished as the club's top [[Try (rugby)|try]] scorer.

In 1998, Critchley switched codes and joined [[rugby union]] side [[Newport RFC|Newport]]. He returned to league during the rugby union off-season to play for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in 2000.<ref>{{cite web |title=A game of two codes |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/world_cup_2000/1025711.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=12 May 2024 |date=16 November 2000}}</ref>

===International career===
Critchley was selected for the [[1996 Great Britain Lions tour]], but did not play in any Test matches.

Following the relaxation of eligibility rules, Critchley qualified to play for [[Wales national rugby league team|Wales]]. He was selected in the 40-man training squad for the [[1995 Rugby League World Cup]], but did not make the final squad.<ref>{{cite web |title=Saints hold Wales' World Cup hopes |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/wales/941149.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=12 May 2024 |date=25 September 2000}}</ref> He made his debut a year later, and went on to win eight [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for Wales between 1996 and 2001.<ref name= "Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> He was selected by Wales for the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]],<ref>{{cite web |title=Tiger Critchley in Welsh RL World Cup squad |url=http://en.espn.co.uk/scrum/rugby/story/32795.html |website=ESPN |access-date=12 May 2024 |date=25 September 2000}}</ref> making five appearances during the tournament, and scoring a try in Wales' 22–8 win in the quarter final against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Wales win sets up Aussie showdown |url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/world_cup_2000/1020204.stm |website=BBC Sport |access-date=12 May 2024 |date=12 November 2000}}</ref>
